[[File:Accursed sceptre spell.gif|thumb|250px|A player using the accursed sceptre's built in spell.]]
The '''accursed sceptre'''  is a [[powered staff]] requiring 70 [[Magic]] to wield. It is a direct upgrade of [[Thammaron's sceptre]], made with level 85 [[Crafting]] by using the [[Skull of Vet'ion]] on the Thammaron's sceptre (note that this process is reversible). Players without the required level to combine the items may ask [[Phabelle Bile]] in [[Ferox Enclave]] to do so instead, for a fee of 500,000 coins. The item counts as a [[Zamorak]]ian item in the [[God Wars Dungeon]].

Compared to [[Thammaron's sceptre]], the accursed sceptre has a higher max hit (usually +1 or +2 higher, depending on gear), +7 extra [[magic accuracy]], and a unique [[special attack]].

The sceptre also has a "Swap" option that allows players to switch it to an [[accursed sceptre (a)]], which is a variant that can [[autocast]] offensive [[combat spells]] from the [[standard spellbook]] (including [[Flames of Zamorak]]) and the [[Ancient Magicks]].

==Passive effect==
When the accursed sceptre is charged with [[revenant ether]], an additional 50% magic accuracy and damage boost is applied when attacking any [[NPC]] in the [[Wilderness]], consuming 1 ether per attack whether the player hits or not. It is also applied to any [[combat spells]] cast when it is equipped, though it cannot [[autocast]] them in powered staff mode.

This effect will stack with a [[Slayer helmet (i)]] or a [[Salve amulet(i)]] and their variants, but not both simultaneously. This makes the sceptre the best magic weapon in the Wilderness, surpassing the damage output of a [[trident of the seas]] or [[Trident of the swamp|swamp]] with the additional capability of attacking any hostile players, which both tridents are incapable of doing.

==Special attack==
[[File:Condemn.gif|thumb|250px|A player performing the Accursed sceptre's special attack, ''Condemn''.]]
The accursed sceptre has a [[special attack]], ''Condemn'', which consumes 50% of the player's special attack energy and 1 [[revenant ether]] (whether it hits or not). The special attack hits an enemy with a 50% increase to max hit and accuracy, and upon a [[successful hit]], also drains the target's Defence and Magic by 15%, regardless of the damage inflicted.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1630218328240209920|date=27 February 2023|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/97025|archivedate=29 March 2023|quote=Looks to me like it's unaffected by what damage you did. It's saying "up to 15%" because if the target's stat was already de-buffed, it'll take them down to a total de-buff of 15%, rather than stacking with the existing de-buff for a bigger total effect.}}

If the target's levels have already been reduced, ''Condemn'' will only drain them to a maximum of 15%. As a result, multiple special attacks do not stack. For example, a target with level 80 Defence and Magic cannot have their stats drained lower than 68 if using Condemn.

==Charging and death==
To use the sceptre's passive effect, it must first be activated with 1,000 [[revenant ether]] (costing {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Revenant ether|1000}}|c}}). The 1,000 ether used to activate the sceptre '''does not''' count towards the usage of the passive effect, thus additional ether must be added (up to 16,000) to increase the sceptre's damage output. Players can configure the charges while the sceptre is in their bank by right-clicking on Configure-Charges, as long as players have the required materials. The hourly cost of constantly using this weapon to cast spells is 1,500 ether or {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Revenant ether|1500}}|c}}, as spells can only be cast every 4 [[game tick]]s.

The ether can be safely removed by selecting the right-click ''Uncharge'' option on the sceptre, after which the sceptre becomes tradeable again.

Upon [[death]] within the Wilderness, any revenant ether stored within the sceptre will '''always''' be dropped, even if it is one of the player's protected items. Because of this, despite the sceptre being able to store a maximum of 17,000 revenant ether, it is recommended to store the minimum amount required for a trip into the Wilderness (in addition to the 1,000 ether required to activate the weapon).

==Maximum hit==
The [[Maximum Magic hit|maximum hit]] dealt by the sceptre is dependent on the player's current [[Magic]] level. The formula is as follows:

<math>\begin{align}
\text{Max Hit} &= \frac{\text{Magic}}{3} - 6\\
\end{align}</math>

The base [[Maximum Magic hit|maximum hit]] dealt by the sceptre is dependent on the player's current [[Magic]] level, starting at 17 with level 70 Magic, increasing by one at 72 and every three Magic levels thereafter, up to a maximum of 35 at level 123 (obtainable only through [[smelling salts]] in [[Tombs of Amascut]]). Both magic damage bonuses and visible stat boosts will modify the max hit of the spell; likewise, stat-reductions will lower the damage. Invisible boosts, such as from the sceptre's attack styles, do not affect the max hit.  At 0 magic, the sceptre has a maximum hit of 0.

==Trivia==
*The accursed sceptre, alongside Thammaron's sceptre, are almost weightless which is unique compared to other powered staves.
